title: paraprasophyllum roseum
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum roseum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Exposure to its sap can also lead to skin rashes and blisters, which may be painful and itchy. Improper use of the plant may also trigger respiratory issues, such as asthma-like symptoms and shortness of breath.
